user_query:
I'm triaging a tricky Android crash/ANR and need a reproducible, locally verifiable incident summary from the attached artifacts. Please process the inputs under input/ and produce structured outputs under output/ as follows:

Inputs to use:
- input/logs/ (scan all *.txt here): Android logcat dump(s) with crashes and an ANR indicator. In this set, process input/logs/logcat_2024-10-05.txt.
- input/anr/ (scan all *.txt here): ANR traces. In this set, process input/anr/traces.txt.
- input/release/mapping.txt: R8/ProGuard class mapping of the form "original.fqcn -> obfuscated.fqcn". Use this to replace obfuscated class names in stack traces with their original names.

Produce all of the following outputs (do not modify inputs):
1) output/deobfuscated_traces.txt
- Contains the deobfuscated stack trace lines extracted from all crash blocks in the logcat files under input/logs/.
- Only include the crash blocks (from the line that starts with "E AndroidRuntime: FATAL EXCEPTION" through the end of that stack block) with each obfuscated class name replaced by the original per input/release/mapping.txt.
- Preserve the order of crashes as they appear in the logs being processed.

2) output/summary/crash_summary.json (UTF-8 JSON) with the following fields:
- total_crashes: integer count of crash blocks identified from the logcat files (count occurrences of "FATAL EXCEPTION").
- exceptions: array of objects sorted by descending count, each {"type": string, "count": integer}, where type is the Java exception class name (e.g., "java.lang.IllegalStateException").
- crash_timestamps: array of ISO-8601 timestamps for each crash in log order, derived from the logcat timestamps (assume year 2024 and format each as YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.mmm using the date in the filename or line; for 10-05 use 2024-10-05). Example: "2024-10-05T14:23:11.234".
- top_app_frames: array aligned with crash_timestamps; for each crash, capture the first stack frame line after deobfuscation whose class belongs to the app (i.e., matches any original class from mapping.txt). Represent each as {"class": "fully.qualified.ClassName", "method": "methodName"}. If no app frame is present, use the first frame in the crash block with whatever name is available after deobfuscation.

Cross-check: the number of deobfuscated crash blocks in output/deobfuscated_traces.txt must equal total_crashes in crash_summary.json, and the number of entries in crash_timestamps and top_app_frames must equal total_crashes.

3) output/anr/anr_report.json (UTF-8 JSON) with the following fields extracted by inspecting input/anr/ and correlating with input/logs/:
- process: the process cmd line from the traces (e.g., "com.example.app").
- anr_timestamp: the timestamp from the traces header if present; otherwise null. For the provided traces, parse the header line with date/time and emit ISO-8601 (e.g., "2024-10-05T14:23:12").
- main_thread_top_obfuscated: the first application frame on the main thread stack that corresponds to an obfuscated class present in mapping.txt (e.g., "r.t.u.b.onReceive").
- main_thread_top_deobfuscated: the same frame after applying mapping.txt (e.g., "com.example.receiver.BootReceiver.onReceive").
- correlated_logcat_line: the exact log line from input/logs that indicates the ANR (the line containing "ANR in com.example.app"), copied verbatim.

Operational requirements:
- Inspect the input directory structure (input/logs and input/anr) rather than hardcoding filenames. Only process *.txt files found there.
- Implement your processing using a script and run it locally to generate all outputs deterministically. Provide a runnable entry point at output/tools/run_report.sh that executes your pipeline end-to-end and regenerates all artifacts above when invoked from the workspace root.
- Do not use the network. Keep everything self-contained.

Verification notes for this dataset:
- There are exactly two crash blocks in input/logs/logcat_2024-10-05.txt and one ANR indicator line. The mapping file includes entries for the obfuscated classes seen in the logs/traces so that deobfuscation is possible. Your outputs should reflect these facts accurately. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
